# Coding Agent Source Index

Converted from `47_coding_agent_source_index.csv` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

| id | title | type | url | why_read | bias_notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| anthropic-opus-47-news | Introducing Claude Opus 4.7 | official announcement | https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7 | Launch narrative, partner claims, Opus 4.7 positioning around long-running coding, agents, vision, and knowledge work. | Vendor source. Good for release facts and claimed improvements; weak for independent model comparison. |
| anthropic-opus-model-page | Claude Opus model page | official product page | https://www.anthropic.com/claude/opus | Product positioning and partner comments for Opus 4.7. | Vendor source; treat quotes as early adopter evidence, not neutral benchmarking. |
| anthropic-models-overview | Claude API models overview | official docs | https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview | Current model IDs, pricing, context window, output limits, model-selection guidance. | Official technical docs; accurate for Anthropic platform facts, not independent performance validation. |
| anthropic-opus-47-whats-new | What is new in Claude Opus 4.7 | official docs | https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/whats-new-claude-4-7 | Breaking API changes, adaptive thinking, xhigh effort, task budgets, image resolution, tokenizer changes. | Official technical docs; strong for implementation details. |
| anthropic-system-cards | Anthropic model system cards | official system card index | https://www.anthropic.com/system-cards | Canonical location for Claude Opus 4.7 System Card and safety/capability evals. | Vendor disclosure document; useful but not independently reproducible by default. |
| claude-code-overview | Claude Code overview | official docs | https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview | Core Claude Code concepts: instructions, skills, hooks, project context. | Official docs; strong for usage features. |
| claude-code-how-works | How Claude Code works | official docs | https://code.claude.com/docs/en/how-claude-code-works | Agentic loop, tools, context, sessions, permissions, checkpoints, branch workflows. | Official docs; strong for architecture and operating model. |
| claude-code-best-practices | Best practices for Claude Code | official docs | https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices | Explore-plan-code-commit workflow, CLAUDE.md, verification, CLI tools, MCP, hooks, skills, subagents. | Official guidance; practical but Anthropic-specific. |
| claude-code-common-workflows | Claude Code common workflows | official docs | https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows | Codebase exploration, debugging, refactoring, testing, PRs, session management. | Official docs; implementation oriented. |
| swebench-main | SWE-bench official leaderboards and benchmark family | benchmark | https://www.swebench.com/ | Canonical software-engineering benchmark family: Verified, Full, Multilingual, Multimodal, Lite. | Public benchmark. Useful, but leaderboard metrics can be harness-sensitive and may not match your repo. |
| swebench-paper | SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues? | paper | https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06770 | Original benchmark paper; explains task format and real GitHub issue setup. | Academic benchmark; important baseline but Python/repo distribution limits generality. |
| swebench-multilingual | SWE-bench Multilingual | benchmark | https://www.swebench.com/multilingual.html | Extends SWE-bench style evaluation beyond Python across 9 languages. | Better language coverage, still benchmark-specific. |
| swebench-pro | SWE-Bench Pro: Can AI Agents Solve Long-Horizon Software Engineering Tasks? | paper/benchmark | https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.16941 | Harder long-horizon coding-agent evaluation; important for production-like claims. | More realistic than basic tasks, still not your production environment. |
| terminal-bench | Terminal-Bench 2.0 | paper/benchmark | https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.11868 | Hard terminal-environment tasks with 89 curated tasks; relevant to CLI/agent workflows. | Good for command-line agency; not a direct proxy for product/marketing/webapp work. |
| aider-polyglot | Aider LLM leaderboards and Polyglot benchmark | benchmark | https://aider.chat/docs/leaderboards/ | Practical code-editing benchmark across C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust. | Tool/harness-specific; good for Aider-like editing, not complete software engineering. |
| utboost-swebench | UTBoost: Rigorous Evaluation of Coding Agents on SWE-Bench | paper | https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.189/ | Shows some SWE-bench tasks have insufficient tests and that leaderboard rankings can change after test augmentation. | Important corrective paper; do not use it to dismiss SWE-bench entirely. |
| data-contamination-survey | A Survey on Data Contamination for Large Language Models | paper | https://arxiv.org/html/2502.14425 | Explains benchmark contamination and mitigation strategies. | Survey; broad rather than coding-agent-specific. |
| llm-benchmark-survey | A Survey on Large Language Model Benchmarks | paper | https://arxiv.org/html/2508.15361 | Catalogues benchmark limitations: contamination, cultural/language bias, missing process credibility and dynamic environments. | Survey; useful for meta-evaluation discipline. |
| swebench-live | SWE-bench Goes Live! | paper/benchmark | https://openreview.net/forum?id=OGWkr7gXka | Live-updatable benchmark built from newer GitHub issues to reduce staleness and contamination. | Promising direction; still benchmark-dependent. |
| prodcodebench | ProdCodeBench: A Production-Derived Benchmark for Evaluating AI Coding Agents | paper | https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01527 | Methodology for building benchmark tasks from real production assistant sessions, including tests and stability checks. | Best read as methodology for your own eval, not as universal benchmark truth. |
| metaculus-metr-productivity | METR / AI productivity studies | research area | https://metr.org/ | Use as a gateway for external work on AI productivity and task completion, especially when benchmark gains do not translate to human productivity. | Research org; check individual study design before generalising. |
